scope: finite-level lifted-operator r=3 reconnaissance over Phase C outputs
method: manual triage of Python wrapper outputs and proof-boundary validator output
claim_boundary: This is finite-level reconnaissance only. It is not a theorem, not a canonical closure, not Lean-ready, and not evidence for any Collatz-level conclusion.
reproduction_command: python3 .agents/skills/collatz-exact-algebra-lab/scripts/run_exact_target.py spectral-fast && python3 .agents/skills/collatz-exact-algebra-lab/scripts/run_exact_target.py modular-unit-fast && python3 .agents/skills/collatz-exact-algebra-lab/scripts/run_exact_target.py modular-full-fast && python3 .agents/skills/collatz-exact-algebra-lab/scripts/run_exact_target.py factor-python && python3 .agents/skills/collatz-exact-algebra-lab/scripts/run_exact_target.py cross-level && python3 .agents/skills/collatz-exact-algebra-lab/scripts/collect_witnesses.py

questions.question: Did spectral-fast reveal repeated unit/full structure across s = 0.50, 0.55, 0.60?
questions.answer: It showed positive numerical S-level gaps for unit and full models at the three slices, with gap sizes decreasing as s increases. No exact repeated symbolic structure was established.
questions.label: Computational Observation

questions.question: Did modular-unit-fast or modular-full-fast produce a stable nonzero determinant witness pattern?
questions.answer: Both probes produced 4/4 nonzero finite-field determinant samples using the same sampled primes and t values.
questions.label: Computational Observation

questions.question: Did factor-python reveal any candidate compact factors, repeated factor templates, quotient decompositions, or residual decompositions?
questions.answer: No. It produced unit/full plan reports stating that Python exact charpoly is blocked by dimension and recommending Sage exact charpoly/factorization.
questions.label: Not Established

questions.question: Did cross-level reveal any invariant pattern worth exact testing?
questions.answer: No concrete invariant was claimed. The output requires defining a candidate invariant and normalization before exact testing.
questions.label: Not Established

questions.question: Which exact Sage target, if any, is justified next?
questions.answer: Run sage-r2 as required sanity, then sage-r3-unit as the smallest exact target aligned with compact-factorization triage. Do not run sage-r3-full unless unit exact output identifies a concrete full-model target.
questions.label: Advisory Only

questions.question: Which claims remain Not Established?
questions.answer: r=3 compact factorization, structural mechanism, subdominant spectral structure, determinant nonvanishing for all real s > 0, cross-level invariance, and Lean-ready theorem candidates.
questions.label: Not Established

questions.question: Which claims should be explicitly rejected or downgraded?
questions.answer: Any upgrade from Phase C outputs to theorem, canonical closure, all-real-s determinant claim, cross-level invariance theorem, or Collatz-level conclusion.
questions.label: Over-Upgraded

candidates.evidence: factor-python wrote reports/r3_unit_factor_search_plan.md and recommended Sage because dimension 18 blocks default Python exact charpoly
candidates.label: Advisory Only
candidates.exact_next_test: timeout 1800 python3 .agents/skills/collatz-exact-algebra-lab/scripts/run_exact_target.py sage-r3-unit after sage-r2 sanity
candidates.risk: May find no factorization; exact output is finite-level only

candidates.evidence: factor-python wrote reports/r3_full_factor_search_plan.md, but no unit exact target has stabilized yet
candidates.label: Not Established
candidates.exact_next_test: Do not run full Sage unless unit exact output identifies a concrete full-model target or user authorizes runtime
candidates.risk: 27-dimensional full target may be expensive and currently lacks a narrowed candidate

candidates.evidence: unit/full modular probes both returned 4/4 nonzero determinant samples over finite fields
candidates.label: Computational Observation
candidates.exact_next_test: No Sage all-real-s test justified from samples alone; possible later determinant-polynomial target requires explicit statement and ring
candidates.risk: Modular samples do not prove positivity or all-real-s nonvanishing

candidates.evidence: r3_spectral_probe.csv shows numerical second absolute eigenvalue and positive gap at three slices for both models
candidates.label: Computational Observation
candidates.exact_next_test: No exact test until a symbolic eigenvalue/factor candidate is specified
candidates.risk: Floating-point eigenvalues do not prove algebraic spectral structure

candidates.evidence: reports/cross_level_invariance_plan.md says no invariant is claimed and requires defining a candidate invariant
candidates.label: Not Established
candidates.exact_next_test: None until candidate invariant and normalization are specified
candidates.risk: Similarity language would over-upgrade without independent witnesses at both levels

promotion_decision.reason: unit is the smallest exact target aligned with compact-factorization triage; full is not justified until unit exact output identifies a concrete full-model follow-up

fixed_path_artifact_risk.note: The wrappers are timestamped, but the underlying experiment scripts rewrite these fixed-path artifacts.
